main.description: A custom emoji. The record key is the canonical alias for ASCII aliases, or its RFC 3492 Punycode encoding (xn-- prefixed) for internationalised aliases; see Bluemoji RFC 0005.

formats.description: Open union to allow for future formats. #formats_v0 is deprecated: writers MUST write #formats_v1 (see RFC 0001 Adoption strategy / RFC 0005). Readers SHOULD continue to accept #formats_v0 for historical records.

stickerFormats.description: Optional full-size renditions for use as a sticker (post attachment via blue.moji.embed.sticker). Presence of this field marks the item as sticker-capable; items with only 'formats' are inline emoji.

labels.description: Self-label values for this emoji. Effectively content warnings.

formats_v0.description: DEPRECATED. apng_128/lottie are raw Bytes (not Blob), which makes them invisible to blob-based image moderation pipelines and requires a getRecord round-trip to render. New writes MUST use #formats_v1. Retained only so existing v0 records remain readable.

stickerFormats_v0.description: Full-size (up to 512×512) renditions for sharing as a sticker. Raster formats should be exactly 512px on the longest edge.

blob_sticker.description: Stickers render one-per-post at full size, so a larger budget than inline emoji is acceptable.

blob_v1.description: Limiting blobs to 128kb because there may be many on page and these get optimised by ImgProxy anyway

blob_v0.description: Limiting blobs to 256kb because there may be many on page and these get optimised by ImgProxy anyway

uri.description: AT-URI of the source item record. Views need this so consumers can derive the owning DID for blob URL construction.

cid.description: CID of the source item record, so consumers can build a com.atproto.repo.strongRef to it (e.g. for moderation reports) without a separate lookup.

did.description: DID of the repo that owns this item's blobs.

copyOf.description: AT-URI of the item this was directly copied from, mirroring the record's own copyOf field.

originalCreator.description: The account at the root of this item's copyOf chain (walked server-side, since a copy can itself be copied), if this item is a copy at all. Omitted for original items. If a link in the chain is missing (e.g. a source account deleted its copy), resolves to whatever the chain reaches rather than failing outright.
